title: Golden Crispy Egyptian Lentil Tagine
description: A flavorful and earthy Egyptian dish featuring crispy lentils and aromatic spices, cooked to perfection in a clay pot. This low-calorie-friendly recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Preheat the clay pot over medium heat.
Add the olive oil, chopped onion, and minced garlic to the pot and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red lentils to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes or until the lentils are tender.
Increase the heat to medium-high and cook, uncovered, for 5 minutes or until the lentils are crispy and golden brown.
Serve the Golden Crispy Egyptian Lentil Tagine hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro.
